top_signals.title: The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover
top_signals.summary:
Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary from across the political spectrum.Every Monday, War on the Rocks members get a curated selection of op-eds and editorials from Ukrainian-language media to understand how Ukrainians themselves debate the biggest issues shaping their country. Western coverage of Ukraine often narrows to the battlefield; these pieces widen the view to daily life, politics, and public argument in a nation at war.A typical edition provides roughly a dozen pieces sorted into five categories: Frontline and Strategy, Allies and Diplomacy, Domestic Politics, Economy, and Society and Culture — closing with detailed Editor’s

The post The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin slides toward $63,000 as Coinbase premium stays negative for a record 60 days
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin fell near $63,000 as chip stocks sold off, the Coinbase premium stayed negative a record 60 days, and ETF flows remain thin.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Ukrainians rally against dismissal of tech-minded defense minister Fedorov
top_signals.summary: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who championed the push to integrate drone technology and digital innovation into the military.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Bank of England appoints Rhys Phillips as the new Chief Cashier
top_signals.summary: The Governor of the Bank of England, Andrew Bailey, has announced that Rhys Phillips will be the next Chief Cashier and Director of Notes. He will take up the role on 19 October 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: The AI rotation stole the spotlight from a strong start to earnings season
top_signals.summary: The AI trade dominated the market once again this week, upstaging an impressive start to the second-quarter earnings season.

top_signals.title: 2 defense tech companies sue US Navy after losing out on MUSV program
top_signals.summary: Two defense tech companies sued the U.S. government, alleging they were unfairly excluded from the Navy's Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, January 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income increased $113.8 billion (0.4 percent at a monthly rate) in January, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $219.9 billion (0.9 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $81.1 billion (0.4 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $85.8 billion in January. Personal saving was $1.05 trillion in January, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of disposable personal income-was 4.5 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Japan ‘cannot avoid’ nuclear weapons debate, defense chief Koizumi says
top_signals.summary: Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi's remarks come as the government weighs revising the country's decades-old pledge not to produce, possess or host nuclear arms.
